rulings.Case Title: CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO, A MUNICIPAL ET AL VS. ANNE KIHAGI ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendant Notice Of Motion To Vacate The Judgment Of May 23 And October 18 2017 For Prosecutorial Misconduct
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for July 14, 2021 line 1. DEFENDANT ANNE KIHAGI Defendant Notice Of Motion To Vacate The Judgment Of May 23 And October 18 2017 For Prosecutorial Misconduct is transferred to department 624 to be heard by the Honorable Angela Bradstreet on August 27, 2021 at 10:00 a.m. =(501/HEK) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303; or Phone Dial in: (415) 762-9988; Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified, and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: HOWARD ROBIN ET AL VS. SOTHEBY'S INTERNATIONAL REALTY, INC. ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for July 14, 2021 line 3. CROSS DEFENDANT ADRIAN BLANCO, NEUMANN SLOAT ARNOLD ARCHITECTS, INC. DEMURRER to 1ST Amended CROSS COMPLAINT is OVERRULED as to all three causes of action, 10 calendar days to answer. "[T]he difficulties in demurring on statute of limitations grounds are clear  when the relevant facts are not clear such that the cause of action might be, but is not necessarily time-barred, the demurrer will be overruled." (Austin v. Medicis (2018) 21 Cal. App. 5th 577, 585). The relevant alleged facts on the negligent misrepresentation and professional negligence claims are not clear whether or not the Robins' March 7, 2018 demand put Schumacher and Piccinini on inquiry notice that the work performed by Neumann Sloat and Blanco was not done in accordance with their alleged representations and was performed negligently. The relevant alleged facts on the breach of contract claim are not clear whether or not Schumacher and Piccinini discovered or reasonably should have discovered that they were harmed by the alleged contract breaches prior to Blanco's deposition on January 30, 2020. =(501/HEK) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303; or Phone Dial in: (415) 762-9988; Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified, and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: TENDERLOIN NEIGHBORHOOD DEVELOPMENT CORPORATION VS. CRAIG GOMEZ ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for July 14, 2021 line 4. DEFENDANT CRAIG GOMEZ Amended DEMURRER to COMPLAINT is OVERRULED, 5 days to answer. Assuming without deciding that 37.9(n) is not preempted and limits the permissible scope of an unlawful detainer claim, plaintiff TNDC sufficiently alleges a claim for unlawful detainer based on violence, threats of violence, and/or health and safety issues. Among other things, the 10 Day notice states that: "you physically prevented  Hailey Stephenson from closing her office's door, thereby trapping Ms. Stephenson and ,,, Maria Maya in said office and causing them to fear for their safety  You  threatened to 'come out swinging' if anyone comes to knock on your door." =(501/HEK) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303; or Phone Dial in: (415) 762-9988; Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified, and the opposing party does not appear.
